item.title: SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son Scraps Plan to Retire in His Sixties
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.’s Masayoshi Son said he plans to hold onto the top spot at the technology group he founded for another decade or more, shredding his long-held plan to hand over the reins in his sixties.

item.title: Conservative Fujimori Poised to Win Peru’s Presidency, Vote Count Shows
item.description: Conservative Keiko Fujimori is poised to become Peru’s next president after running her fourth consecutive campaign, according to figures published by the elections regulator after weeks of adjudicating disputed ballots.

item.title: India Lets NRIs Leverage Deposits to Draw Dollars
item.description: RBI swap covers only principal, not interest.

item.title: AirPods Maker Luxshare Gauges Interest for $3 Billion Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Electronics manufacturer Luxshare Precision Industry Co. has started gauging interest for a Hong Kong listing that may raise about $3 billion, according to people familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified to discuss a private matter, moving toward what could be one of the city’s biggest deals this year.

item.title: China Weakens Yuan Fixing for Fourth Session as Dollar Advances
item.description: China’s central bank set the daily guidance rate for the yuan weaker for a fourth straight session, showing flexibility in the currency management to the US dollar’s advance.

item.title: Bank of Korea Says Higher Rates Needed Amid Housing, Debt Risks
item.description: South Korea’s central bank reinforced its hawkish shift, saying higher interest rates will be needed at an appropriate time as rising home prices, household debt and leveraged investment threaten to stoke financial imbalances.

item.title: Malaysia Central Bank Pledges Stronger Measures to Boost Inflows
item.description: Malaysia will intensify measures to encourage foreign-exchange inflows, including efforts to boost the repatriation and conversion of companies’ overseas earnings, the central bank said.

item.title: BOJ Summary Affirms Rate Hike Stance as Inflation Risks Mount
item.description: The Bank of Japan signaled the need for further increases to the benchmark interest rate in a summary of opinions from a last week’s board meeting, when authorities raised the policy rate to the highest since 1995.

item.title: Australia’s Core Inflation Accelerates, Keeping RBA on Alert
item.description: Australia’s core inflation remained above the top of the Reserve Bank’s target in May, reinforcing expectations policymakers will maintain a hawkish stance on interest rates.

item.title: Bessent Signals Confidence in Warsh, Sees Inflation Coming Down
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent signaled confidence in newly installed Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh during remarks Tuesday, and predicted that inflation will retreat as the Iran conflict subsides.

item.title: Sanrio Shares Seesaw After Full-Year Forecast Beats Estimate
item.description: Sanrio Co. shares briefly jumped the most since February after the Japanese Hello Kitty owner forecast full-year operating profit above analyst estimates.

item.title: Korean Stocks Rebound After 10% Rout as Focus Back on Earnings
item.description: South Korean stocks rebounded from a 10% rout, with investors refocusing on chipmakers’ earnings after what many saw as a selloff driven by technicals.

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX Adds Billions in Debt While Cutting Interest Costs
item.description: Elon Musk is no stranger to financial alchemy. His latest feat: adding billions of dollars of debt onto his cash-burning business empire while simultaneously cutting its annual interest burden.

item.title: Pimco Sees China’s Export Glut Boosting Emerging-Market Bonds
item.description: China’s flood of cheap exports is bolstering the case for emerging-market bonds by helping to keep inflation under control across the developing world, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

item.title: Thailand Seen Holding Rate to Support Growth as Neighbors Hike
item.description: Thailand is expected to keep its key interest rate unchanged at a nearly four-year low to support its fragile recovery, bucking a tightening trend among some regional peers.

item.title: Gold Drops Below $4,100 as Tech-Led Selloff Spurs Liquidation
item.description: Gold extended a decline, as a tech-led selloff on Wall Street prompted investors to cut bullion holdings to cover losses elsewhere in their portfolios.

item.title: UK Issues Energy Supply Warning as Heat Wave Strains Power Grid
item.description: The UK’s grid operator issued a rare summer power supply warning for Wednesday evening as soaring temperatures put stress on the energy system.

item.title: Bessent Warns Against US Being at ‘Mercy’ of Foreign Chokepoint
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said US economic statecraft policies will be guided in part by ensuring the nation addresses vulnerabilities to any adversary being able to curtail vital supplies.

item.title: Xi Pressures Takaichi by Throttling Key Mineral Exports to Japan
item.description: China is choking shipments of some critical minerals to Japan, a slowdown that’s hurting companies and prompting calls for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to find a diplomatic off-ramp with Beijing.

item.title: German Rail Halted Nationwide Services After Radio System Outage
item.description: Germany’s national rail operator temporarily halted services countrywide late on Tuesday after an outage of its communication system.

item.title: UAE Oil Exports Surge to 85% of Pre-War Levels, IEA Says
item.description: Oil exports from the United Arab Emirates in early June recovered to nearly 85% of the country’s pre-Iran war levels — rebounding even before Washington and Tehran inked an interim peace deal as the Gulf nation drew on pipelines, storage and alternate shipping routes, according to a report from the International Energy Agency.

item.title: Asian Stocks Set to Extend AI Chip-Fueled Selloff: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were poised to extend Tuesday’s losses after a bruising Wall Street tech-led selloff deepened concerns that the AI-driven equity rally has run too far.

item.title: Oil Holds Drop as More Tankers Cross Hormuz After Peace Talks
item.description: Oil held declines as more tankers openly cross the Strait of Hormuz, with the US and Iran making progress toward ending the war.

item.title: Ship Insurers Set for Major Claims From Iran War, Allianz Says
item.description: The insurance industry is set to face significant claims for ships that were damaged during the Iran war, according to Allianz SE.

item.title: China’s Instagram Gets Ready to Go Public
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, we look at Xiaohongshu’s push to go public and what it reveals about confidence in China’s tech sector.

item.title: Colombia Gas Reserves Stay Near 18-Year Low as Shortfall Deepens
item.description: Colombia’s natural gas reserves remained near their lowest level in almost two decades in 2025, increasing pressure on the nation to secure additional import capacity as a domestic shortfall deepens.

item.title: Meta’s Cox Sought Shah’s WhatsApp Advice — Then Made Him Leader
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc.’s decision to hand leadership of WhatsApp to Kunal Shah, one of India’s most prominent angel investors and the founder of fintech startup Cred, started with a cold email.

item.title: MSCI Upgrades Bulgaria to Frontier; Vietnam, Argentina Unchanged
item.description: MSCI Inc. reclassified Bulgaria as a frontier market, while indicating no changes to Argentina’s and Vietnam’s status in its latest review of global stock indexes.

item.title: Anthropic Customer Sues US Over Losing Access to Fable AI Model
item.description: An artificial intelligence startup that uses Anthropic PBC’s AI models sued the US over a government order that the maker of Claude and Mythos keep its most advanced technology out of the hands of foreign nationals.

item.title: MSCI Maintains Korea’s Emerging Market Status in Latest Review
item.description: MSCI Inc. kept South Korea in its emerging-market index and refrained from starting a formal review process that could eventually elevate the $5 trillion stock market to developed-market status.
